questions.question: Which two organizations jointly released the Universal Health Coverage (UHC) Global Monitoring Report 2025?

options.label: A
options.text: World Health Organization and United Nations International Children’s Emergency Fund

options.label: B
options.text: World Health Organization and World Bank Group

options.label: C
options.text: United Nations Development Programme and World Bank

options.label: D
options.text: International Monetary Fund and World Health Organization

answer.label: B
answer.text: World Health Organization and World Bank Group

questions.explanation: UHC Global Monitoring Report 2025 was jointly released by the World Health Organization (WHO) and World Bank Group. The report analyses global progress towards Universal Health Coverage (UHC), tracking access to essential health services and financial protection. It highlights improvements in health service coverage and reductions in financial hardship due to out-of-pocket (OOP) health expenses since 2000. The report identifies persistent inequalities, with the poorest and most vulnerable populations facing the greatest barriers to UHC. It provides recommendations for governments, including expanding public health investments, reducing OOP costs, strengthening primary care, and promoting equitable access to essential services.

questions.question: Which institute has launched India’s first incubator-linked deep-tech venture capital fund?
questions.explanation: IIT Bombay’s Society for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (SINE) has launched India’s first incubator-linked deep-tech venture capital fund, named the Y-Point Venture Capital Fund, with a total corpus of ₹250 crore. The fund aims to provide early-stage risk capital to India’s deep-tech startups, especially those emerging from IIT Bombay and other top research institutions across the country. It will invest in pre-seed and seed-stage companies, helping them translate cutting-edge research into commercially viable technologies. The fund plans to support 25–30 startups, offering investments of up to ₹15 crore to each selected company.

questions.question: What is the name of the AI-powered tool launched by the government for automated summarisation of Gram Sabha and Panchayat meetings?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Panchayati Raj informed that 92,376 Gram Panchayats have used the SabhaSaar tool for automated meeting summarisation across States and Union Territories. A standard operating procedure (SOP) was shared with States and Union Territories, which was further disseminated to Gram Panchayats for proper adoption. It was launched on 14 August 2025. SabhaSaar is an AI-enabled voice-to-text tool that automatically summarises meetings, improving efficiency and record-keeping in local governance.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book “The Art of Doing Nothing Right”, released in December 2025?
questions.explanation: Elan Group, with Invincible Publishers, launched “The Art of Doing Nothing Right”, debut book by Ajit Navraj Hans, on 9th December 2025 at Elan Epic Mall, Gurugram. The event was attended by Daler Mehndi, Hans Raj Hans (Padma Shri), and Navraj Hans, celebrating creative legacy and family inspiration. The book explores embracing imperfections, learning from mistakes, and celebrating authenticity and resilience.

questions.question: India launched its first hydrogen-powered water taxi service in which city?
questions.explanation: India launched its first hydrogen-powered water taxi service in Varanasi on December 10, 2025. The service was inaugurated by Union Minister of Ports, Shipping & Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal, marking a major advancement in India’s clean transport initiatives. The water taxi has been built by Cochin Shipyard, integrating modern engineering with sustainability. It operates on a hybrid electric–hydrogen propulsion system, ensuring zero emissions, low noise levels and high environmental safety. The vessel is equipped with five hydrogen cylinders and 3 kW solar panels, which help meet auxiliary power needs.

questions.question: Which state is home to India’s first Power Museum?
questions.explanation: Bihar is set to establish the country’s first Power Museum, which will be developed on a 3-acre campus of the decommissioned Karbigahiya Thermal Power Station in Patna. The museum aims to preserve Bihar’s power-sector heritage, promote tourism, and function as a centre for educational and industrial research. The transformation of the historic Karbigahiya power station into a state-of-the-art museum is expected to highlight the evolution of electricity generation and distribution in India, offering Patna a unique tourist attraction.

questions.question: Bar-headed goose, that was recently seen in news, is native to which region?
questions.explanation: A first-of-its-kind study in eastern India tracked a bar-headed goose using a GSM–GPS transmitter, revealing its migration route and flight pattern. The bar-headed goose is a migratory bird known as one of the highest-flying birds in the world. It can fly at altitudes of up to 25,000 feet while crossing the Himalayas, surviving extremely low oxygen levels and freezing temperatures. The species is native to Central Asia, where it breeds, and is found across India, Pakistan, Nepal, Kazakhstan, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Japan, and nearby regions. In India, its distribution extends from the northeastern region to southern parts of the country. It prefers high-altitude lakes during breeding and freshwater lakes, rivers, and streams during wintering.

questions.question: International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialised agency of which organization?
questions.explanation: The Government of India recently showcased the country’s pioneering achievements in rural transformation and development leadership at the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) – India Day event held in Rome, Italy. The event highlighted India’s successful policies, programmes, and innovations aimed at eradicating rural poverty, strengthening livelihoods, and transforming rural economies. International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialised agency of the United Nations. Its primary objective is to eradicate poverty and hunger in rural areas of developing countries by empowering rural communities.

questions.question: Centralised Public Grievance Redressal and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) is developed and monitored by which organization?

options.label: A
options.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG)

answer.label: A
answer.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG)

questions.explanation: India’s Centralised Public Grievance Redressal and Monitoring System has reduced pending public grievances by 74 percent since 2021. Centralised Public Grievance Redressal and Monitoring System is a 24×7 online platform for citizens to file service delivery complaints. It is developed and monitored by the Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances, and Pensions. It works as a single portal connected to all Union and State government ministries and departments. Citizens can access it through the website, a mobile app, and the Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance.

questions.question: What is the name of the mission launched by government to create a comprehensive digital database of livestock and related services across the country?
questions.explanation: The Government has launched the National Digital Livestock Mission (NDLM) to create a comprehensive digital database of livestock and related services across India, as informed to Parliament. The mission aims to improve livestock productivity, breed improvement, disease surveillance, traceability of livestock products, and farmer-centric service delivery. So far, about 9.5 crore livestock owners have been registered and 35.96 crore animals have been issued Pashu Aadhaar. The digital backbone of NDLM is the Bharat Pashudhan platform, supported by mobile and web applications for field workers.
